title: Wire voucher_check into Dash as a capability
description: Import checkVoucher/issueVoucher directly from core-brain or copy functions. Register in Dash server.ts. Dash already has its own FileSystemLongTermMemory instance.

title: Fix Dash greeting — 'running locally' is misleading
description: LLM inference hits OpenRouter/Anthropic. Rewrite to be accurate about what's local (data) vs cloud (inference).

title: Fix Dash model label — model name tag missing in UI
description: Model name tag (e.g. claude-sonnet-4) missing next to agent name in UI. Was showing before, now gone. UI regression.

title: Harden airplaneMode into real privateMode
description: Currently just swaps provider to Ollama. Needs to actually block all outbound LLM API calls at the request layer. Fail loud if Ollama isn't available.

title: Audit git history for PII
description: Run git log -p scan on both core and dash-brain repos for personal data, credentials, env vars. Squash or filter-branch before anything goes public.

title: Add access audit logging
description: When any brain file is read via MCP or direct access, log it (who, what, when). Currently no trail of who accessed what.

title: Make .locked enforcement consistent
description: MCP server respects it, but Dash reads files directly and bypasses the lock check entirely. Centralize the guard.

title: Minimal HTTP server — random port + mDNS announce
description: Done: random available port on startup. Still needed: announce via mDNS so Dash (or any instance) can discover and render Core's system board.

title: Claude Code as orchestrated agent
description: Dash spawns claude --dangerously-skip-permissions as child process. Governance replaces permission prompt: voucher token, locked paths, append-only JSONL heartbeat. Dash terminates if agent goes silent or drifts.

description: When any brain file is read via MCP or direct access, log it (who, what, when). Currently no trail of who accessed what.

title: [bottleneck] Long-running system task for auto-decision updates
description:
**Auto-escalated from insight engine**

**Category:** bottleneck
**Confidence:** high
**Discovered:** 2026-03-04T22:53:51.219Z

**Problem:** The long-running system task for auto-decision updates is causing a bottleneck, potentially dragging other checklist indexes further due to queued activity.

**Investigation steps:**
1. Read the current status of the system task (queued or running) by reading brain/tasks/active.json
2. Check the last modification time of brain/checklist_indexes/mcp.json to determine if it has been updated recently
3. Verify if other checklist indexes are pending (brain/checklist_indexes/*.pending.json) and due for processing
4. Compare the timestamps of brain/checklist_indexes/*.pending.json files to determine if they are indeed being dragged further by the long-running task

**Root cause hypothesis:** The long-running system task is causing a bottleneck due to centralizing.locked file enforcement across MCP and Dash, dragging other checklist indexes further and due multiple work alongside.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- The system task is not queued (it's either running or completed)
- The last modification time of mcp.json is recent (within the past hour)
- No pending checklist indexes are due for processing (all files have a due date in the past)
- The timestamps of pending checklist indexes show that they are not being dragged further by the long-running task

**Files to check:**
- `brain/tasks/active.json` — to determine current system task status
- `brain/checklist_indexes/mcp.json` — to check for recent updates
- `brain/checklist_indexes/*.pending.json` — to identify affected checklist indexes

_Insight ID: ins_1fcd43f7_

title: [anomaly] Agent Timed Out: Audit Git History
description:
**Auto-escalated from insight engine**

**Category:** anomaly
**Confidence:** high
**Discovered:** 2026-03-04T23:04:02.219Z

**Problem:** The Agent Audit git history for PII across repos has timed out multiple times, indicating a performance or resource issue with the git history retrieval process.

**Investigation steps:**
1. Read the Brain/config/repos-scan.yml file to verify the expected git history URLs
2. Check the Brain/logs/agent/git-history.log file for error messages or clues about the failure
3. Verify the repositories listed in Brain/config/repos-scan.yml have up-to-date credentials and are accessible
4. Compare the timestamps of the agent activities to identify any patterns or simultaneous events

**Root cause hypothesis:** One of the repositories listed in Brain/config/repos-scan.yml has a problem with its configuration or connection

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Verify the Agent can successfully audit git history for PII across all repos without timing out
- The response time for the Agent's git history retrieval should be < 30 seconds
- The Agent should not time out on any repo within the next 5 attempts

**Files to check:**
- `Brain/config/repos-scan.yml` — configuration file for git history retrieval
- `Brain/logs/agent/git-history.log` — log file for agent activities

_Insight ID: ins_ec97d534_

title: [bottleneck] Delayed Agent Audit and Backlog Review
description:
**Auto-escalated from insight engine**

**Category:** bottleneck
**Confidence:** high
**Discovered:** 2026-03-04T23:14:07.424Z

**Problem:** The audit agent is timing out on audit Git History, causing a significant delay in backlog review, resulting in an increased backlog review time of approximately 11237ms.

**Investigation steps:**
1. Verify the audit history report for file src/brain/audit.history by checking its timestamp to ensure it was generated after the audit agent timed out.
2. Check the wall clock time of file src/brain/audit.timestamp to determine if the audit agent was still running within its maximum allowed runtime, as per the configuration in brain/config/runtime.config
3. Analyze the contents of the audit batch file src/brain/audit.batch to see if it contains any pending or partially completed audit entries that might be contributing to the delay

**Root cause hypothesis:** The audit agent is timing out due to an excessively long-running audit task or an issue with the audit history report generation process.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Audit history report files are not being generated within 30s of audit agent invocation
- wall clock time in audit timestamp file is greater than the audit runtime configured in brain/config/runtime.config
- Audit batch file contains no more than 10 pending or partially completed audit entries

**Files to check:**
- `src/brain/audit.history` — generated audit history report in response to audit agent execution
- `src/brain/audit.timestamp` — contains wall clock time of audit agent execution

_Insight ID: ins_42eccca5_

title: [bottleneck] Investigate audit agent timeout bottleneck
description:
**Auto-escalated from insight engine**

**Category:** bottleneck
**Confidence:** high
**Discovered:** 2026-03-04T23:24:16.380Z

**Problem:** Audit agent investigation timeouts exceeding 2 minutes, requiring human input, indicating potential issues with core agent configuration or functionality.

**Investigation steps:**
1. Review the core agent configuration file (src/brain/agent_config.py) for any recent changes or updates.
2. Check the audit agent logs (src/logs/audit_agent.log) for error messages or warning signs that may indicate the root cause of the timeout.
3. Compare the timestamp of the audit agent timeout with the planning timestamp to determine if there's a correlation between the two events.

**Root cause hypothesis:** The audit agent timeout bottleneck may be caused by a misconfigured or overwhelmed audit agent, leading to increased investigation time and requiring human input.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Investigation completion time < 1 minute
- Number of errors in audit agent logs = 0

**Files to check:**
- `src/brain/agent_config.py` — recent changes or updates to agent configuration may impact investigation timing
- `src/logs/audit_agent.log` — error messages or warning signs in the audit agent logs may indicate the root cause of the timeout

_Insight ID: ins_9a92262f_

title: [bottleneck] Investigate git history audit timeout
description:
**Auto-escalated from insight engine**

**Category:** bottleneck
**Confidence:** high
**Discovered:** 2026-03-04T23:24:16.380Z

**Problem:** Git history audit timeouts are causing bottlenecks, requiring human input and exceeding 2-minute processing times.

**Investigation steps:**
1. Read the system log entry from src/logs/system.log at timestamp '2026-03-04T23:14:07.424Z' to gather more information about the bottleneck
2. Check the file src/services/audit/git_history.go for any recent changes or potential performance issues
3. Compare the audit timeout threshold ( src/settings/audit_threshold.json ) with the actual response times recorded in src/logs/audit.log

**Root cause hypothesis:** A misconfigured audit threshold or recent changes in the git history audit service might be causing the bottleneck, leading to extended processing times and human input requirements.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- The audit process completes within 5 seconds
- No more than 1 human input request is required during the audit process

**Files to check:**
- `src/services/audit/git_history.go` — Recent changes in this file might be causing the bottleneck
- `src/settings/audit_threshold.json` — Adjusting this threshold might resolve the performance issue

_Insight ID: ins_9519ed4d_

title: [bottleneck] Long-running Span between Inspection and Auto-commit
description:
**Auto-escalated from insight engine**

**Category:** bottleneck
**Confidence:** high
**Discovered:** 2026-03-04T23:34:21.799Z

**Problem:** Significant delay between judgment and auto-commit steps, potentially indicating a processing bottleneck.

**Investigation steps:**
1. Read the judgment step in the training scan's trace (brain/trace/ts_96962de0b9d1.json) to confirm the long-running span
2. Check the response time metric for the auto-commit step in the same trace
3. Compare the timestamps of the judgment and auto-commit steps to quantify the delay

**Root cause hypothesis:** Insufficient system resources (CPU, memory) causing a bottleneck in the processing pipeline between judgment and auto-commit steps

**Acceptance criteria:**
- The response time for the auto-commit step is greater than 10s
- The delay between judgment and auto-commit steps is consistently greater than 5s

**Files to check:**
- `brain/trace/ts_96962de0b9d1.json` — Contains the judgment and auto-commit steps for the relevant training scan
- `brain/trace/ts_96962de0b9d1.json` — Contains the auto-commit step's response time metric

_Insight ID: ins_a27dbaa1_

title: [bottleneck] Large Span between Config scan and Batch Completion
description:
**Auto-escalated from insight engine**

**Category:** bottleneck
**Confidence:** high
**Discovered:** 2026-03-04T23:34:21.799Z

**Problem:** Long processing delay between batch session start and completion, causing potential system downtime.

**Investigation steps:**
1. Read the 'batch.log' file in the 'brain/records/session_<timestamp>' directory to confirm the span duration
2. Check the 'Config.scan' activity timestamp in the 'src/activity.log' file to verify scan completion time
